title: Pokemon Detail Lookup, Derived Views, Units, and Provenance · Failure Repair Bay

objectives: Accept numeric ID or constrained canonical name, retain requested and resolved identity, and reject ambiguous or hostile input.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 8383 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate Pokemon detail fields, unit conversions, ordered presentation, and provenance; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify request identity, response identity, source URL, cache state, unit conversion, sorting, missing fields, and changed fixtures.

Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-detail-provenance-tests-bo6mr9" that uses case 3330 to validate Pokemon detail fields, unit conversions, ordered presentation, and provenance. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.title: Evidence defense
